meta_title: For Founders: debugging with AI to Solve tutorial hell (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome tutorial hell. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Debugging with AI to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Break free from tutorial hell. Learn to debug with AI using Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your SaaS MVP development and ship faster.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop consuming and start shipping. By integrating Antigravity and Claude Code into your workflow, you transform your IDE into an autonomous pair programmer. This shift allows you to iterate on real business logic instead of getting stuck in endless, abstract syntax tutorials.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks watching video tutorials, tweaking boilerplate code, and feeling like you’re making progress—only to freeze the moment you face a real-world bug. This is 'tutorial hell,' the silent killer of SaaS startups. It’s a comfortable trap where you feel productive without ever shipping a product users will pay for.

Real engineering isn't about knowing every syntax; it's about solving business problems at velocity. By leveraging AI-driven debugging, you bypass the learning curve and move directly into execution. We are going to treat your code as a living product that evolves through intelligent, AI-assisted iteration, not passive observation.

steps.title: Define the Business Constraint
steps.description: Before writing code, clearly define the technical hurdle preventing your MVP from reaching production. AI needs context, not just generic error logs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: I am building a SaaS MVP. My current blocker is [Insert Problem]. Analyze this as a senior engineer and provide the most efficient path to resolution that prioritizes speed-to-market.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Analyze my current project structure and identify the primary bottleneck in my authentication flow.'

steps.title: Automated Bug Reproduction
steps.description: Use Claude Code to isolate the failing logic. Stop manually testing and let the AI generate the reproduction suite to confirm the bug.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a test script that reproduces this specific error: [Insert Error]. Ensure the test is isolated and focuses on the core business logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a test file to reproduce the bug in the database connection string logic.'

steps.title: Applying Surgical Fixes
steps.description: Instead of rewriting files, apply targeted patches. AI debugging is about minimizing regression risks while maintaining velocity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Apply a surgical fix to the identified bug. Prioritize clean, production-ready code that adheres to standard SaaS architecture patterns.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Refactor the data validation service to handle empty state inputs without crashing the API.'

steps.title: Systemic Resilience Audit
steps.description: Don't just fix the bug; harden the system. Use Antigravity to audit the surrounding code for similar patterns that could cause future downtime.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a resilience audit on the current module. Identify potential race conditions or performance bottlenecks that could hinder scaling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review the current codebase for potential scaling issues and suggest architectural improvements for high-concurrency users.'

steps.title: Deployment Verification
steps.description: Verify the fix within the staging environment to ensure the user journey remains uninterrupted.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a checklist for verifying this fix in a production-like environment. What edge cases should I monitor during the next deployment?
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Verify the fix by simulating a high-traffic user login event in the local environment.'

pro_tips: Always provide the AI with your 'Definition of Done' to prevent over-engineering.
Use Claude Code to document your changes automatically in your README to keep your technical debt visible.
If the AI suggests a major library change, ask for a 'Migration Impact Analysis' before executing.
Treat your AI prompts like a team sprint task: be concise, provide context, and define the expected output.
Debug during your peak focus hours; treat AI as your intern, not your manager.
content.next_steps: Now that your MVP is bug-free, focus on your first 10 user interviews. Do not add new features until you have verified your core value proposition with real revenue.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer a student; you are a founder. Stop debugging in circles and start building a business that scales. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
